# AGENDA ITEM III.1a
## Regular Board Meeting
### Approved Minutes
#### May 19, 2025

**District Attendees**  
Misha Sarkovich - Board President  
Randy Marx - Board Vice President  
Mark Dolby - Board Member  
Chris Petersen - Board Member  
George Babcock - Board Member  
Shawn Huckaby - Operations Manager  
Chi Ha-Ly - Finance Manager  
Paul Siebensohn - Water Supply Superintendent  
Rebecca Simon - Human Resource Administrator  

**Other Attendees**  
Paul Helliker - SJWD General Manager  
Ted Costa - SJWD Board President  
Michael McRae - SJWD Board Vice President  

**Absent**

---

## AGENDA ITEMS

### I. CALL TO ORDER
- President Sarkovich called the meeting to order at 6:30 p.m.

### II. PUBLIC COMMENT
- An unknown visitor requested that FOWD do some digging on Pennsylvania Avenue, it needs an overlay like New York Avenue; it’s amazing! He needs an overlay like that, it’s beautiful. He shared an incident he had with a water filter he replaced on his refrigerator. The water filter blew up, causing a mess. There was water everywhere. He believes the water pressure blew up the filter.  
Director Sarkovich agreed with the comment regarding the overlay. He stated it was very well done. It was worth the wait.

### III. CONSENT CALENDAR
The following consent calendar items were considered and acted upon as follows:  
1. Approval of Minutes  

---

Page 1 of 12
<!-- Page 2 -->
- Regular Board Meeting of April 21, 2025
1. Accept and File Treasurer’s Report for the month of April 2025
2. File Investment Report for the month of March 2025
3. Accept and File Financial Expense Report for the month of April 2025
   - Director Petersen noted that on page 25, under the Water Supply item line, it reflects FOWD spent a lot more on SJWD’s water than FOWD budget to date. He went back to look at the water supply report and noticed that FOWD has not used that much more than budgeted volumes and would like to know the cause. FOWD has spent almost 50% of the annual budget on water through April.
   - Financial Manager Ha-Ly stated that FOWD pays in advance for the fixed cost of the water, on a quarterly basis.
   - President Sarkovich noted that 83% of the bill is at a fixed rate.
   - Director Petersen appreciated the clarification.
4. Approval of Warrants
5. Approval of Cal-Card Statements for the month of April 2025
6. Approval of Board Expense Report for the month of April 2025
   - President Sarkovich moved to approve the consent calendar as presented.
   - Vice President Marx seconded the motion.
   - Motion carried with the following votes: Babcock – aye, Dolby – aye, Marx – aye, Petersen – aye, and Sarkovich – aye.

IV. PRESENTATIONS AND CORRESPONDENCE
1. None

V. DISCUSSION AND ACTION ITEMS: OLD BUSINESS
1. Update and discussion on pre-purchasing steel casing required to construct the FOWD Board approved Northridge Well
   - Information only.
   - Operations Manager Huckaby noted that this agenda item was approved at the last Board Meeting. There is a warrant in the Board packet for the pre-purchase of this item. He stated that the contractor will be responsible for purchasing and storing the material.
   - Director Petersen stated we won’t know casing or screen exact length until the exploratory drilling is done at its location. This is a replacement well, therefore FOWD knows how the old well was built. He inquired if any unused steel can be sent back.
   - Water Supply Superintendent Siebensohn replied that he did not know.
   - Director Petersen stated that exploratory boring is first and then the materials are ordered to figure final well design. He knows in this case FOWD had good reason.
<!-- Page 3 -->
![Page 3 of 12](https://via.placeholder.com/768x993.png?text=Page+3+of+12)

to pre-purchase. He stated that we are not going to know exact lengths until FOWD gets through that exploratory drilling.
- Operations Manager Huckaby stated that what FOWD is purchasing is based on an estimate. He asked if the Board is asking if FOWD can return the materials.
- Director Petersen replied yes.
- Operations Manager Huckaby stated he believed it was discussed at the last Board Meeting but does not remember the answer.
- Director Petersen stated that sometimes it’s worth having a discussion with the driller because they just take materials back to their yard and use them for a future well project. It may be worth having the discussion in case the material quantity on the final well design is different than what FOWD estimated for this pre-purchase.

2. Update and discussion on the draft Water Forum Agreement 2025
- Operations Manager Huckaby noted there is a copy of the draft agreement in the Board packet.
- SJWD General Manager Helliker apologized for missing the FOWD Board discussion at the last meeting. He asked if the Board went over the document.
- President Sarkovich stated that FOWD didn’t go into great detail. They did not have the draft last month, just talked about it.
- SJWD General Manager Helliker stated they are closing in on completing the agreement. This project started five years ago partly because the original agreement expires in 2030. So, we’re all continuing to operate under that agreement. Two things were happening: Tom Gohring was about to retire, former Executive Director, and he wanted to finish the update before his retirement but ended up retiring sooner than expected. The other component is the Healthy Rivers & Landscapes Program, which FOWD participates via the Artesian Agreement. We had been expecting it (agreement) to come to fruition sooner than it has. The goal at this point is to have it this year, all the components to be completed beyond the Artesian Agreement which was one of the first components. Those were two things that were part of the reason that we started this project five years ago, ten years before the agreement terminates. The past five years have been spent updating the agreement and negotiating some new language from agreement. The document found in tonight’s packet is close to final. SJWD has a meeting with the Environmental Caucus in two weeks to go over some of the things they would like. There are now many, many things that are new, that were not in place back in 2000 (i.e. Urban Water Management Plans, Groundwater Sustainability Plans, CIGMA, Conservation Regulations, and three rounds of conservation programs since 2000). So, a lot of things are different now than they were back in 2000. During that time (in 2000), there was a lot of concern about large amounts of growth and increase water demand. At this point SJWD is not projecting a lot of increase in water demand – at least not in the SJWD service area. But we have the Purveyors Specific Agreement, a joint agreement with Citrus Heights Water District (CHWD), FOWD, Orangevale, Folsom Ashland area. Actually, SJWD agreement is not included in that document (in Board packet), it’s going to be added in the next round of the revision. That part of the agreement talks about projects that SJWD would like to
<!-- Page 4 -->
![Page 4 of 12](data:image/png;base64,
<!-- Page 5 -->
- Director Petersen inquired if every water agency completes one of these Purveyors Specific Agreement, including FOWD.
- SJWD General Manager Helliker stated that FOWD has a joint Purveyors Specific Agreement with SJWD. The focus of the Water Forum Agreement is on surface water convergence. This time around though, there is a much more robust discussion about groundwater use. The Purveyors Specific Agreement is a joint document that was prepared in conjunction with FOWD, CHWD, Orangevale and Folsom. It includes our joint commitment and our projections of what the future surface water demand will be. He stated that General Manager Gray has a copy of that since he has been actively involved in helping to shape it. He stated that he is sure General Manager Gray will give Director Petersen his own copy or SJWD General Manager Helliker is happy to send it to Director Petersen. He thanked FOWD for their partnership in the agreement.
- SJWD Vice President McRae stated that he has been trying to wrap his brain around the Water Forum Agreement, it is very confusing. The agreement as he understands it, is that FOWD would be different than SJWD would have. He does not fully understand it at this time but he wants to share with FOWD some of his concerns. Is signing this agreement a net benefit for FOWD ratepayers? And is it legal? If there is a net cost to FOWD ratepayers and if he understands California law correctly, one can only charge customer owners the cost to deliver water to them. One cannot charge them to fight global warming or warm rivers and save fish, etc. Those are things he is trying to wrap his brain around to share those concerns with FOWD.
- SJWD General Manager Helliker stated that the agreement is the same for every signatory and SJWD’s Purveyor Specific Agreement will be one of the components of the Water Forum Agreement, as well as the other Purveyor Specific Agreements. In terms of the cost, Sacramento County had been funding through their Zone 13 taxes, The contribution that FOWD, CHWD, Orangevale, SJWD and Sacramento County would have been providing over the past 20-25 years. They notified SJWD just a few years ago that they will be phasing out their contribution. If FOWD has not already, you’ll be looking at an inner-agency agreement for a contribution from FOWD of $60,000 or so, he thinks it is the number. Since SJWD will be paying for San Juan retail, Orangevale, and Folsom Ashland, they will be paying a bit over $100,000. Total among all the San Juan family members is about $250,000 and that is out of the total budget for the Water Forum of about $2 million. The Water Forum has been a very critical part of the regional water picture for the past 25 years and it will be for the next 25 years. The Water Forum will be implementing the habitat component of our healthy rivers and landscape proposal, which FOWD and SJWD are participating in. So, we need the Water Forum to continue. The contributions we are making are certainly, I think a reasonable amount. With respect to how it relates to water rates. Water rates are based upon recovering the costs of operations. SJWD’s cost of operations is composed of a whole host of different expenses, one of which is the Water Forum, and the other one is the contributions for the Healthy Rivers and Landscapes Program. That’s all part of the SJWD expense picture, which state law allows SJWD to allocate to their customers. But it’s that allocation that is covered by Prop 218, it has to be allocated in a manner that doesn’t give any  
Page 5 of 12
<!-- Page 6 -->
![Image](https://via.placeholder.com/768x993.png?text=Image+Not+Available)

VI. DISCUSSION AND ACTION ITEMS: NEW BUSINESS  
1. Discussion on FOWD Water Supply for the month of April 2025  
   - Water Supply Superintendent Siebensohn provided a summary of the water supply report.

2. Discussion and possible action on additional funding for 2025 expenses  
   - Finance Manager Ha-Ly recommended the Board to authorize General Manager Gray to transfer $1,100 from the contingency fund to cover 2025 expenses for office equipment rental.  
   President Sarkovich moved to authorize General Manager Gray to transfer $1,100 from the contingency fund to cover 2025 expenses as detailed.  
   Vice President Marx seconded the motion.  
   Motion carried with the following votes: Babcock – aye, Dolby – aye, Marx – aye, Petersen – aye, and Sarkovich – aye.

3. Discussion and possible action on FOWD Policy No. 1010 – “Conflict of Interest”  
   - Operations Manager Huckaby presented the Board with the updated Policy No. 1010 “Conflict of Interest” that reflects the title change from Technical Services Manager to Water Supply Superintendent.  
   President Sarkovich moved to approve the updated Policy No. 1010 – “Conflict of Interest,” as presented.

VII. UPCOMING EVENTS  
1. May 21, 2025 / SJWD Board Meeting / SJWD Office  
2. May 27, 2025 / RWA Executive Committee Meeting / Sacramento  
   - Vice President Marx attended their meeting about a week ago, the good news is that they will keep FOWD’s rates the same.  
3. May 29, 2025 / SJWD Special Board Meeting: Retail Water Rate Workshop / SJWD Office  
4. June 11, 2025 / SJWD Special Board Meeting: Public Hearing on Retail Water / SJWD Office  
5. June 12, 2025 / SJWD Special Board Meeting / SJWD Office  
6. June 12, 2025 / SGA Board Meeting / Sacramento

1. Sacramento Groundwater Authority (SGA)  
   - None.  
2. Regional Water Authority (RWA)  
   - None.  
3. Sacramento Water Forum  
   - None.  
4. Other  
   - None.  

IX. DIRECTORS’ REPORTS & COMMENTS  
1. Budget Committee – (Sarkovich, Marx)  
   - None. The Committee did not meet.  
2. Technical Advisory Committee – (Marx, Petersen)  
   - None. The Committee did not meet.

3. Capital Improvement Committee – (Sarkovich, Petersen)  
   • None. The Committee did not meet.

4. Personnel Committee – (Babcock, Dolby)  
   • None. The Committee did not meet.

5. Public Relations Committee – (Babcock, Dolby)  
   • None. The Committee did not meet.

6. FOWD and SJWD 2x2 Ad-Hoc Committee – (Petersen, Marx)  
   • None. The Committee did not meet.

X. GENERAL MANAGER’S REPORT  
1. Maintenance Work Report  
   • Report provided.

2. Capital Projects Status Report  
   • Report provided.  
   • Director Petersen pointed out the 2025 Approved Budget column, totaling $9.7 million. Most people would look at that and believe that FOWD will be implementing $9.7 million of capital improvements in 2025 because that is the approved budget for 2025. What he has learned about being on the Board is that FOWD does not really stick to that. We are a third of the way into the year, and a third of $9.7 million would be over $3 million spent if we are actually on budget. This would be one way to interpret it, but to date FOWD has only spent $800,000. A person studying this table would say that FOWD is way behind on their capital improvements, and that could make FOWD look bad. The way FOWD operates its capital program is having a series of projects to do in a year, but that does not match the first column. There seems to be a disconnect. He proposes no action tonight, but this is a discussion item. He would like to see FOWD closely tie a budget for the year to what FOWD will actually do that year. This will allow the Board to track it and be on the same page. If FOWD is not on budget, there would be a dialogue with staff.  
   • President Sarkovich stated that sometimes it is out of FOWD’s control, like the paving on Lemon St., which was delayed due to weather conditions.  
   • Director Petersen inquired if FOWD would be capable of spending the $9.7 million approved for 2025.  
   • President Sarkovich responded no.  
   • Director Petersen stated that there is a disconnect that needs to be unraveled by the Board, to provide more transparency to the ratepayers on what FOWD is doing.  
   • President Sarkovich stated that the Corporate Yard Project has a budget for 2025, but FOWD is still waiting on building permits to get approved.  
   • Director Petersen stated that he would like to see staff’s best estimate of the projects they believe are feasible to implement in any given year and drive.  

Page 8 of 12
<!-- Page 9 -->
![Page 9 of 12](attachment://image.png)

- President Sarkovich inquired if this would be done monthly and commented about deviations during the year.
- Director Petersen stated he is aware there will be deviations, but he also knows that every year he’s been on the Board, FOWD never comes close to spending what is allocated in an approved budget year. Sometimes FOWD only spends about 25%. It is a disconnect that has always bothered him.
- Vice President Marx inquired if Director Petersen believes it is because money is not being allocated by year properly to the project or overly conservative.
- Director Petersen responded yes. He stated that staff is not planning by project in the capital program. It is not tied to specific projects. It’s a bag of money that sits in the capital program.
- Vice President Marx commented he agrees with Director Petersen.
- Finance Manager Ha-Ly commented that the budget allocation is by project.
- Director Petersen stated that it might be tied to specific projects, but FOWD is never even close to implementing those projects in any year. The four years he’s been on the Board, FOWD has been far off; spending only 25% of that annual budget. There’s a giant disconnect, and we need to get a grip on that.
- An unknown visitor stated that he believes the way FOWD is managing the budget is probably the only way FOWD can do it, otherwise it would be too much work. He has done earned value and it’s not fun.
- Director Petersen believes FOWD has a lot more room for discipline.
- President Sarkovich stated that there were cases of state budgeting examples where it’s the end of the year and according to their budget, they have to spend so many thousands of dollars and it’s a mad dash to spend money just because it may not be there next year.
- Director Petersen stated he is not in a mad dash to spend money, but he stated, let’s not every year approve a budget FOWD knows it will never come close to spending. It does not make sense, and he doesn’t want that in writing anywhere.
- President Sarkovich stated that it’s worse to start a project without properly planning funding. At least FOWD has a place holder and know the money is there if FOWD starts the project.
- Director Petersen asks if President Sarkovich is okay with FOWD having a budget for $9.7 million for this year and to date $800,000 has been spent.
- President Sarkovich replied yes, as long as he knows why.
- Director Petersen asks why he would be okay with that.
- Director Sarkovich stated because he knows why. For example, the Lemon Street project FOWD was supposed to spend all the money by the end of 2024 and then it started raining.
- Director Petersen stated that FOWD then has to document exactly why FOWD is not spending it. He does not think it’s a good way of doing business. FOWD can do better than that.
<!-- Page 10 -->
- Vice President Marx agrees that the budget should reflect what is budgeted to be spent that year and believes it should be within a 30-50% range, but it’s off by more.
- Finance Manager Ha-Ly stated probably this budget year staff will look closer at what capital projects can really be accomplished.
- Operations Manager Huckaby provided New York Well as an example. The paving could not be finished. There are project-assigned funds that FOWD does not get to spend wherever they want, it belongs to specific projects. Money cannot be moved around and be spent on other things without coming to the Board first.
- Director Petersen stated that in capital planning he has seen, it will show an expenditure by year until all capital projects are built; cannot tell by looking at FOWD report. Another way to handle it is to plan our expenditures with number of years to complete.
- Finance Manager Ha-Ly mentioned that the T-Main Replacement Construction Project has a $2.5 million budget and construction is starting this month, along with other projects that will be starting in May 2025.
- Operations Manager Huckaby noted that at times FOWD is further along in the project, but expenses have not been incurred yet; therefore, it is not being reflected on the report.
- Further discussion took place among the Board and staff about spending more time polishing capital improvement budget and planning this summer and fine tuning it.
- Operations Manager Huckaby stated that the iPads need to be updated, and he would like to collect them any time after the meeting. He will hand them back at the next Board Meeting. He also noted that the FOWD website has the Board’s FOWD emails for customers to contact them directly and encouraged the Board to check their emails regularly as those emails are not coming to FOWD.

3. Authorizations of Additional Funding
- Report provided.

4. Water Transfer Status Report
- None.

5. Claims Against District
- Report provided.

6. Employee Update
- Director Dolby inquired if there were any new hires.
- Human Resource Administrator Simon stated that there was a new hire for Distribution System Operator I (DSO I). He does not have a distribution certificate but has a lot of heavy equipment experience. He’s already fitting in well. 

Page 10 of 12
<!-- Page 11 -->
- Operations Manager Huckaby stated having a difficult time with the new requirements to get Class A licenses, two operators passed their test this week.
- Director Petersen pointed out that FOWD’s website does not show any current open positions. It seems that having those positions listed on the website would be beneficial for people who are looking for work and they could see what’s available.
- Human Resource Administrator Simon stated that if any recruitments are open, those are posted on the website. However, they are taken down once the position is filled. There are no open positions at the moment, but the Assistant General Manager (AGM) position will be up soon.
- President Sarkovich inquired if the AGM hiring process is starting all over again.
- Human Resource Administrator Simon responded yes. FOWD is already in the process of restarting this. Once the position is open, it will be posted on the FOWD website. This allows FOWD to receive applications for specific open positions.
- Director Babcock suggested adding a box that reads “no open positions at this time.” When a position is open, the applicant can easily scroll down to see it.
- Human Resource Administrator Simon responded she believes there is already something like this on the website and if there isn’t, it will be added. She will check to make sure it’s there.

7. Water Issues – Update on Regional Involvement
- None.

8. Other
- None.

XI. PUBLIC COMMENT
- SJWD Vice President McRae stated he found the legal reference he mentioned earlier. It’s on SJWD’s website, Section 6 Article 13d, Part 4: “No fee or charge may be imposed for a service unless that service is actually used by, or immediately available to, the owner of the property in question.” He stated that this is what he is trying to balance at San Juan. FOWD has groundwater and potential to maybe make money with the Artesian Agreement, so maybe it is something that would pay for itself, have a positive cash flow or maybe it would be negative. He stated that these are things he is concerned about and is sharing with FOWD.

XII. ADJOURNMENT
With no further business to come before the Board, President Sarkovich adjourned the meeting at 7:19 p.m.
<!-- Page 12 -->
The Board approved the preceding minutes on June 16, 2025

Tom R. Gray  
General Manager/Board Secretary
